title: Proofpoint TAP Malicious URL Messages with Incomplete Link Rewriting
dataSources: Proofpoint TAP SIEM message events `sourcetype=proofpoint_tap_siem` (URL threat class)
app: Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk (Splunkbase 3681)
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint_tap_siem" earliest=-24h
| eval rewrite=coalesce(completelyRewritten, CompletelyRewritten, "")
| eval ttype=coalesce(threatType, ThreatType, "")
| where rewrite="false" AND (ttype="URL" OR match(lower(_raw), "(?i)url"))
| stats count values(subject) as subjects by sender, recipient, rewrite
| sort -count
description: When Proofpoint reports malicious URL-laden messages where `completelyRewritten` is false, at least one dangerous link was not rewritten—users may still reach live phishing or exploit pages.
value: Prioritizes incidents for messaging engineering and SOC where URL Defense did not fully neutralize content, reducing reliance on mailbox rules alone.
implementation: Requires TAP SIEM message (blocked or delivered) events with `completelyRewritten` populated. Validate `threatType` extraction—some builds require `mvexpand` on `threatsInfoMap`. Pair with click telemetry (`proofpoint:click`) for the same GUID when available. Alert on any non-`na` rewrite failure in strict tenants.
visualization: Table (sender, recipient, subject samples), single value (events per hour), timeline.

references.title: Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk
Proofpoint TAP SIEM API — message fields

knownFalsePositives: Delayed rewrites on large messages, path MTU oddities, and link scanners timing out once.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.
